title: How to setup a conduit.rs Matrix server
snippet: Deploy using dockerThis tutorial will be for deployments using docker.We will need 2 containers. First a caddy webserver and the other a conduit.rs matrix server.configure caddyFor caddy we can use...
content: Deploy using dockerThis tutorial will be for deployments using docker.We will need 2 containers. First a caddy webserver and the other a conduit.rs matrix server.configure caddyFor caddy we can use caddy:alpine official docker image.the Caddyfile to use:matrix.example.com {	reverse_proxy /_matrix/* <conduit-container-ip>:6167	header /.well-known/matrix/* Content-Type application/json	header /.well-known/matrix/* Access-Control-Allow-Origin *	respond /.well-known/matrix/server `{"m.server": "matrix.example.com:443"}`	respond /.well-known/matrix/client `{"m.homeserver":{"base_url":"https://matrix.example.com"},"m.identity_server":{"base_url":"https://identity.example.com"}}`}the "m.identity_server" is not mandatory.now run the container:docker run -d --network host --restart unless-stopped \-v /PATH/TO/Caddyfile:/etc/caddy/Caddyfile \--name caddy caddy:alpineconfigure conduitnow for the conduit container.. first create a conduit.toml config file. sample could be found at the official conduit repo at https://gitlab.com/famedly/conduit/Then we launch the container:docker run -d --restart unless-stopped \-v db:/var/lib/matrix-conduit/ \-v /PATH/TO/conduit.toml:/etc/matrix-conduit/conduit.toml \-e CONDUIT_CONFIG="/etc/matrix-conduit/conduit.toml" \--name conduit matrixconduit/matrix-conduit:latestAll done!

title: Things to do on a fresh linux installation
snippet: Create new sudo userfirst we create the usersudo adduser madmanthen add it to sudoerssudo usermod -aG sudo madmannow we check if the user can run all commandssudo -l -U madmanDisable root loginwe d...
content: Create new sudo userfirst we create the usersudo adduser madmanthen add it to sudoerssudo usermod -aG sudo madmannow we check if the user can run all commandssudo -l -U madmanDisable root loginwe disable root logins with this commandsudo passwd -l root This will lock the password for the root user and you won’t be able to access the root account with its password until a new one is set.Update and Upgrade the systemsudo apt-get update -y && sudo apt-get upgrade -yand reboot the server (important).Configure Automatic Upgradesfirst install unattended-upgradessudo apt-get install unattended-upgradesthen we reconfigure itsudo dpkg reconfigure --priority=low unattended-upgradesthen we can check the config filesudo nano /etc/apt/apt.conf.d/20auto-upgradesInstall SSH server & configure key-based authenticationInstall ssh serverwe use openssh-server as our ssh-serversudo apt-get install openssh-serverconfigure key based authenticationnow we have to create an ssh key-pair on both the server and client machine.to do that we have to run this command:ssh-keygenbasically the key based authentication works by copying the client’s ssh public key to the server’s authorized_keys file.to do this the easy way we just run this simple command:ssh-copy-id <user>@<server-address>this will automatically copy the client’s public key to the server’s trusted keys over ssh connection.if we want to do this manually we have to copy the contents of the client’s public key to the server’s authorized_keys file.on the client machine:cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa.puband we copy the output.then on the server:nano ~/.ssh/authorized_keyspaste and we’re done.Disable password based authenticationnow that we have enabled key-based authentication it’s logical that we disable password-based authentication for extra security.To do this have to edit the ssh daemon configuration file.sudo nano /etc/ssh/sshd_configUncomment these 2 fields and set them to no:PasswordAuthentication noChallengeResponseAuthentication noalso to disable root account login:PermitRootLogin nonow we restart ssh daemonsudo systemctl restart sshdConfigure Static IPsudo nano /etc/netplan/01-netcfg.yamlnetwork: version: 2 renderer: networkd ethernets: ens18: dhcp4: no addresses: - 192.168.0.222/24 gateway4: 192.168.0.1 nameservers: addresses: [192.168.0.4]apply the settingssudo netplan applyhostnamecheck hostname:hostnamectlchange hostname to rocketshipsudo hostnamectl set-hostname rocketshipmust also change in this file:sudo nano /etc/hostsTimezonecheck timezone:timedatectlchange the timezone:sudo timedatectl set-timezone America/Chicagochange with menu:sudo dpkg-reconfigure tzdata Firewallallow outgoing traffic by default:sudo ufw default allow outgoingdeny incoming traffic by default:sudo ufw default deny incomingallow ssh service:sudo ufw allow sshenable the firewall:sudo ufw enablecheck status:sudo ufw status

title: cloudflare workers scripts
snippet: Aggregated some scripts that I use on cloudflare workers.Reverse ProxyaddEventListener( "fetch", event => { let url = new URL(event.request.url); url.host = "example.com"; let request ...
content:
Aggregated some scripts that I use on cloudflare workers.Reverse ProxyaddEventListener( "fetch", event => { let url = new URL(event.request.url); url.host = "example.com"; let request = new Request(url, event.request); event.respondWith( fetch(request) ) })General Reverse Proxy with this format “/host/path”export default { async fetch(req) { try { const url = new URL(req.url); const splitted = url.pathname.replace(/^/*/, '').split('/'); const address = splitted[0]; url.pathname = splitted.slice(1).join('/'); url.hostname = address; url.protocol = 'https'; return fetch(new Request(url, req)); } catch (e) { return new Response(e); } }}Load Balance 301 Redirectingconst statusCode = 301;const urls = ["https://example1.com", "https://example2.com", "https://example3.com", "https://example4.com" ];const random = Math.floor(Math.random() * urls.length);const base = urls[random]console.log(base);async function handleRequest(request) { const url = new URL(request.url) const { pathname, search } = url const destinationURL = base + pathname + search return Response.redirect(destinationURL, statusCode)}addEventListener("fetch", async event => { event.respondWith(handleRequest(event.request))})Get your IP AddressaddEventListener('fetch', event => { event.respondWith(handleRequest(event.request))})async function handleRequest(request) { let content = request.headers.get('cf-connecting-ip') + '
' return new Response(content, { headers: { "content-type": "application/json;charset=UTF-8", },})}IP details by CloudflareaddEventListener('fetch', event => { event.respondWith(handleRequest(event.request))})async function handleRequest(request) { let content = "{" + '
' content += ' ' + '"IP": "' + request.headers.get('cf-connecting-ip') + '",
' content += ' ' + '"Organization": "' + request.cf.asOrganization + '",
' content += ' ' + '"ASN": "' + request.cf.asn + '",
' content += ' ' + '"Country": "' + request.cf.country + '",
' content += ' ' + '"City": "' + request.cf.city + '",
' content += ' ' + '"Continent": "' + request.cf.continent + '",
' content += ' ' + '"Colo": "' + request.cf.colo + '",
' content += ' ' + '"Latitude": "' + request.cf.latitude + '",
' content += ' ' + '"Longitude": "' + request.cf.longitude + '",
' content += ' ' + '"PostalCode": "' + request.cf.postalCode + '",
' content += ' ' + '"MetroCode": "' + request.cf.metroCode + '",
' content += ' ' + '"Region": "' + request.cf.region + '",
' content += ' ' + '"RegionCode": "' + request.cf.regionCode + '",
' content += ' ' + '"Timezone": "' + request.cf.timezone + '",
' content += ' ' + '"Protocol": "' + request.cf.httpProtocol + '",
' content += ' ' + '"tlsCipher": "' + request.cf.tlsCipher + '",
' content += ' ' + '"tlsVersion": "' + request.cf.tlsVersion + '",
' content += ' ' + '"Method": "' + request.method + '",
' content += ' ' + '"Host": "' + request.headers.get('host') + '",
' content += ' ' + '"UA": "' + request.headers.get('user-agent') + '",
' content += ' ' + '"Language": "' + request.headers.get('accept-language') + '"
' content += "}
" return new Response(content, { headers: { "content-type": "application/json;charset=UTF-8", },})}IP details by ipinfo.ioasync function handleRequest(request) { const clientIP = request.headers.get("cf-connecting-ip") console.log(clientIP) const url = new URL(request.url) let response = await fetch("https://ipinfo.io/" + clientIP + "?token=00000000000000") //enter your token instead of this let text = await response.text() text = text + "
" console.log("THIS IS THE FUCKING LOG!! 
" + text) var init = { "status" : 200 , "statusText" : "OK" }; var response2 = new Response(text,init); return response2}addEventListener("fetch", event => { event.respondWith(handleRequest(event.request))})DNS over HTTPS handleraddEventListener('fetch', function(event) { const { request } = event const response = handleRequest(request) event.respondWith(response)})async function handleRequest(request) { const doh = 'https://cloudflare-dns.com/dns-query' const contype = 'application/dns-message' const { method, headers, url } = request const { host, searchParams } = new URL(url) if (method == 'GET' && searchParams.has('dns')) { return await fetch(doh + '?dns=' + searchParams.get('dns'), { method: 'GET', headers: { 'Accept': contype, } }); } else if (method == 'POST' && headers.get('content-type')=='application/dns-message') { return await fetch(doh, { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Accept': contype, 'Content-Type': contype, }, body: await request.arrayBuffer() }); } else { return new Response("", {status: 404}) }}
